scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. developer.todoist.com/rest/v2/ is a client-side-rendered reference page; ground truth for this review is a community-maintained OpenAPI 3.1 document for this exact API (github.com/aaddrick/GPT-Actions, servers: https://api.todoist.com/rest/v2 — matching this bundle's base_url exactly), cross-checked against the API's own documented example request/response bodies embedded in that document. It declares 28 operations across 13 paths, covering every resource family Todoist's REST v2 API exposes: projects, sections, tasks (+close/reopen), comments, labels, and project collaborators. Every GET (list) resource is now a stream; every single-record GET is excluded as duplicate_of its list stream (same schema, no new data surface); every create/update/delete mutation is now a write action, since Todoist's write endpoints (unlike e.g. Timely's) accept a flat top-level JSON body — no engine dialect gap blocks any of them. The OAuth token-exchange endpoint is out of scope as authentication plumbing, not application data.

excluded.reason: single-project detail returns the identical Project schema already present on every 'projects' stream record sharing the same id; no new data surface
single-section detail returns the identical Section schema already present on every 'sections' stream record sharing the same id; no new data surface

single-task detail returns the identical Task schema already present on every 'tasks' stream record sharing the same id (an ACTIVE task; a completed task is simply absent from both the list and the detail lookup); no new data surface
single-comment detail returns the identical Comment schema already present on every 'comments' stream record sharing the same id; no new data surface
single-label detail returns the identical Label schema already present on every 'labels' stream record sharing the same id; no new data surface
OAuth authorization-code token exchange is authentication plumbing (this connector uses a pre-issued personal API token/OAuth bearer token, sourced out of band), not an application data resource
